ia, Berkeley, CA 94720 November 23, 1998 ABSTRACT The Ptolemy project studies heterogeneous modeling and design of concurrent systems. The focus is on embedded systems, particularly those that mix technologies, including for example analog and digita

hnologies, including for example analog and digital electronics, hardware and software, and electronics and mechanical devices (including MEMS, microelectromechanical systems). The focus is also on systems that are complex in the sense that they mix

stems that are complex in the sense that they mix widely different operations, such as signal processing, feedback control, sequential decision making, and user interfaces. An older Overview can be found here . Send comments to Edward A. Lee at eal@e
und here . Send comments to Edward A. Lee at eal@eecs.berkeley.edu .
